data.title: Pineapple Financial expands share buyback to $15M, immediate repurchases to commence
data.description:
Pineapple Financial approved an expanded $15 million share repurchase program and will commence repurchases immediately under the initial authorization.

Key Highlights:

  • Board increased buyback authorization from $3.0M to up to $15.0M of common shares.
  • Repurchases to begin immediately under the existing $3.0M authorization, subject to laws and absence of material non-public info.
  • Additional up to $12.0M may be repurchased over time, subject to further Board approval and solvency requirements.
  • Timing, price and amount of repurchases at company discretion; program may be modified, suspended or terminated.

Original SEC Filing: Pineapple Financial Inc. [ PAPL ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Oceaneering Reports Q1 2026: Revenue $692.4M, Operating Income $57.8M, Net Income $36.1M
data.description:
Oceaneering reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $692.4 million and operating income of $57.8 million, compared with $674.5 million and $73.5 million a year earlier. Net income was $36.1 million and adjusted EBITDA was $83.7 million; diluted EPS was $0.36 and adjusted diluted EPS was $0.30. The company noted strong Aerospace and Defense Technologies activity, $1 billion of orders in the quarter and maintained full-year EBITDA guidance of $390 million to $440 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $692.4 million for Q1 2026, up 3% year-over-year (Q1 2025: $674.5 million).
  • Gross profit: $127.3 million (gross margin shown in statement; gross profit figure provided in release).
  • Operating income: $57.8 million for Q1 2026, down 21% year-over-year (Q1 2025: $73.5 million).
  • Net income: $36.1 million for Q1 2026, down 28% year-over-year (Q1 2025: $50.4 million).
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $83.7 million for Q1 2026, a 13% decrease versus Q1 2025; diluted EPS $0.36, adjusted diluted EPS $0.30.

Business Highlights

  • Orders and backlog: Generated approximately $1 billion of total orders in Q1 2026, including over $300 million in Subsea Robotics awards and $175 million in Aerospace and Defense Technologies awards; Manufactured Products backlog at $492 million as of March 31, 2026.
  • Subsea Robotics (SSR): Revenue increased to $214.3 million, while ROV utilization declined to 61% (down from 67% a year earlier); ROV revenue per day utilized rose to $12,401 reflecting pricing mix and discrete first-quarter items.
  • Manufactured Products: Revenue up 6% with operating income rising to $26.1 million and margin expanding to 18%, driven by higher‑margin backlog execution and strong Rotator valve performance; 12‑month book-to-bill of 0.91.
  • Offshore Projects Group (OPG): Revenue declined 18% reflecting typical seasonality and lower international activity; operating income decreased and margin fell to 14%.
  • Integrity Management & Digital Solutions (IMDS) and ADTech: IMDS faced lower volumes in West Africa and Australia and Middle East activity was flat due to regional conflict; ADTech revenue grew 35% to $131.2 million with increased submarine repair and maintenance activity but operating income impacted by an accrual related to a contract dispute.

Original SEC Filing: OCEANEERING INTERNATIONAL INC [ OII ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Neuraxis increases ATM capacity to $11.5M after $6.27M program sales
data.description:
Neuraxis amended its at-the-market offering to raise the capacity to $11,500,000 after selling $1.125M worth of common stock under the program.

Key Highlights:

  • Amended ATM prospectus supplement on April 21, 2026 to increase aggregate offering capacity to $11,500,000.
  • Through April 20, 2026, sold 1,125,281 shares under the ATM Agreement with Craig-Hallum.
  • Original ATM permitted up to $6,270,000 in aggregate offering price under Form S-3 registration.
  • Legal opinion regarding issuance and sale attached as Exhibit 5.1 to the Form 8-K.

Original SEC Filing: Neuraxis, INC [ NRXS ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

data.title: Maze Therapeutics prices $150M registered offering; expects ~$144.7M net proceeds, funds into 2029
data.description:
Maze Therapeutics priced a registered offering of 5,540,000 shares and 850,000 pre-funded warrants, expected gross proceeds of $150.0 million.

Key Highlights:

  • Offering: 5,540,000 shares at $23.50 and 850,000 pre-funded warrants at $23.499 each.
  • Net proceeds: Company estimates approximately $144.7 million after underwriting discounts and expenses.
  • Use of proceeds: Primarily advance R&D for MZE829 (APOL1-mediated kidney disease) and MZE782 (PKU, CKD); general corporate purposes.
  • Cash runway: Combined with existing cash and securities, proceeds are expected to fund operations into 2029 under current plan.
  • Underwriter and timing: Leerink Partners sole underwriter; offering expected to close on or about April 23, 2026, subject to customary conditions.

Original SEC Filing: Maze Therapeutics, Inc. [ MAZE ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Jabil Board keeps John Plant and Tiger Tyagarajan after rejecting resignations
data.description:
Jabil retained directors John Plant and Tiger Tyagarajan after rejecting their conditional resignations following the 2026 annual meeting vote.

Key Highlights:

  • The Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee recommended rejecting the resignations of Plant and Tyagarajan; the Board concurred.
  • Both directors had not received majority votes at the 2026 meeting but did not participate in the committee’s review or Board decision.
  • Board cited attendance (over 75% in fiscal 2026 to date), engagement, qualifications and contributions in deciding to retain them.
  • Plant and Tyagarajan will continue serving on the Board and committees through the 2027 annual meeting unless they resign or are removed.

Original SEC Filing: JABIL INC [ JBL ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Contango Announces $9.0M Peak Gold JV Distribution, $63M+ 2026 Exploration Program
data.description:
Contango received a $9.0 million cash distribution from the Peak Gold JV and outlined a fully funded, multi‑project 2026 exploration and infrastructure program.

Key Highlights:

  • Peak Gold JV paid Contango $9,000,000 in cash on March 25, 2026, providing near‑term liquidity.
  • Lucky Shot: 5,900m underground drilling completed; additional 12,100m planned as part of a $21M spend toward a H1 2027 Feasibility Study.
  • Johnson Tract: $17M 2026 budget for 3‑mile access road, camp winterization, portal prep and FAST‑41 environmental permitting.
  • Kitsault Valley: New MRE due end of Q2 and a planned 40,000m surface drill program starting in June as part of a $25M campaign.

Original SEC Filing: Contango Silver & Gold Inc. [ CTGO ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: BIO-key International Received Notice of Noncompliance
data.description:
On April 16, 2026, the company received notice from Nasdaq that it is not in compliance with Listing Rule 5250(c)(1) due to failing to timely file its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025. The notice has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of its common stock on the Nasdaq Capital Market.

Original SEC Filing: BIO KEY INTERNATIONAL INC [ BKYI ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

data.title: Las Vegas Sands Corp. Q1 Profit Climbs
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Las Vegas Sands Corp. (LVS) released earnings for its first quarter that Increases, from last year

The company's bottom line totaled $567 million, or $0.85 per share. This compares with $352 million, or $0.49 per share, last year.

Excluding items, Las Vegas Sands Corp. reported adjusted earnings of $612 million or $0.91 per share for the period.

The company's revenue for the period rose 25.3% to $3.585 billion from $2.862 billion last year.

Las Vegas Sands Corp. earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $567 Mln. vs. $352 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $0.85 vs. $0.49 last year.

-Revenue: $3.585 Bln vs. $2.862 Bln last year.

data.description:

The mean expectation of four analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 76 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 74 cents to 80 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 28.3% to $246.45 million from a year ago; analysts expected $256.42 million.

  • TowneBank's reported EPS for the quarter was 45 cents​.

  • TowneBank shares had risen by 5.9% this quarter and gained 6.8% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 7.6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the banks peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for TowneBank is $41.00, about 13% above its last closing price of $35.65

data.title: Oceaneering Files 8K - Operations And Financial Condition >OII
data.description:
Oceaneering International Inc. (OII) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on April 22, 2026.

On April 22, 2026, Oceaneering International, Inc. ("Oceaneering" or "we") issued a press release announcing Oceaneering's earnings for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026. A copy of that press release is furnished as Exhibit 99.1 to this report and is incorporated by reference into this item 2.02.

The information furnished pursuant to this Item 2.02, including Exhibit 99.1, shall not be deemed to be "filed" for the purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), or otherwise subject to the liabilities of that section, nor shall such information be deemed incorporated by reference into any registration statement or other filing under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Exchange Act, unless specifically identified in such filing as being incorporated by reference in such filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/73756/000007375626000080/oii-20260422.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/73756/000007375626000080/0000073756-26-000080-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: CVB Financial Q1 net interest income rises
data.description:
Overview

  • California commercial bank reported Q1 net income nearly flat yr/yr, EPS rose to $0.38

  • Net interest income grew 6.7% yr/yr, driven by higher average earning assets and loan yields

  • Company completed Heritage Commerce Corp acquisition on April 17, results to be included in Q2

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific financial guidance for the current or future periods in the press release

Result Drivers

  • NET INTEREST INCOME GROWTH - Higher average interest-earning assets and increased loan yields drove 6.7% yr/yr growth in net interest income

  • LOWER FUNDING COSTS - Decreased cost of funds and deposits supported margin expansion

  • HIGHER CREDIT LOSS PROVISION - Provision for credit losses increased due to higher specific reserves for one commercial and industrial credit

data.title: ServiceNow Posts 22% 1Q Revenue Growth, But Says Armis Deal Will Weigh on Margins
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

ServiceNow posted 22% revenue growth in the first quarter but projected the integration of Armis weighing on its margins this year.

The cloud-based software company said Wednesday that the acquisition of Armis, a cybersecurity startup, will result in a roughly 75 basis point headwind to its operating margin for the full year, as well as a roughly 125 basis point headwind to its operating margin in the second quarter.

It is also projecting 25 basis point headwind to full-year subscription gross margin and approximately a 200 basis point headwind to free cash flow margins for the year.

The company guided for full-year subscription revenue between $15.74 billion and $15.78 billion.

That projection fell short of the $15.98 billion Wall Street analysts are expecting, according to FactSet.

"While we will see some near-term headwinds to margins as we integrate the business in FY 2026, strong AI efficiencies internally from Now on Now and our underlying platform leverage are expected to normalize our operating and free cash flow margin expansion trajectories in FY 2027," the company said.

Shares of ServiceNow tumbled 12% in after-hours trading to $90.20. The stock closed up 2.9% at $103.07, down 33% this year.

For the current second quarter, ServiceNow is expecting subscription revenue to grow to roughly $3.82 billion, with current remaining performance obligations expected to rise 19%.

Analysts are expecting $3.86 billion in revenue.

"The early close of our Armis acquisition meaningfully expands our TAM and accelerates our subscription revenue growth trajectory," Chief Financial Officer Gina Mastantuono said.

ServiceNow recorded a first-quarter profit of $469 million, or 45 cents a share, compared with a profit of $460 million, or 44 cents a share, a year earlier.

On an adjusted basis, ServiceNow posted earnings of 97 cents a share, in line with the expectations of analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue rose to $3.77 billion from $3.09 billion, ahead of analysts' projections of $3.75 billion.

"As new technologies create both opportunity and risk, our two decades of engineering combined with deep business context enable us to orchestrate and secure the agentic enterprise," ServiceNow Chief Executive Officer Bill McDermott said. "With this foundation, our AI growth is far exceeding even our own expectations, reinforcing our position as one of the fastest growing enterprise software companies ever."

The company ended the quarter with $12.64 billion in current remaining performance obligations, up nearly 23% year-over-year.

Subscription revenues rose 22% to $3.67 billion. Subscription revenue growth faced a 75 basis point headwind from the delayed closings of several Middle East deals, which were disrupted by the war in Iran, the company said.

ServiceNow's outlook "reflects a prudent assessment of those geopolitical headwinds on deal timing," the company said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: S&P and Nasdaq Set Fresh Records After Cease-Fire Extension — WSJ
data.description:
By Hannah Erin Lang

Stock investors are willing to bet the worst of the U.S. war with Iran is in the past.

All three major U.S. stock indexes climbed Wednesday after President Trump said late Tuesday that he would extend a cease-fire with Iran. The S&P 500 notched its eighth record close of 2026.

The gains extend a rally that has propelled the index to multiple all-time highs in recent trading sessions, even as Iran attacks ships in the Strait of Hormuz and a more permanent peace between the two nations remains far from certain.

The president's cease-fire extension "doesn't really bring any clarity, but for now it's good enough for the market," said Adam Turnquist, chief technical strategist at LPL Financial. "Risk appetite is back."

The S&P 500 rose 1%, while the Nasdaq composite jumped 1.6%, closing at its fourth record high of the year. The Dow industrials advanced 0.7%, or 341 points.

Oil traders, on the other hand, don't appear so convinced. Global benchmark crude prices rose 3.5% to $101.91 a barrel Wednesday, settling above $100 for the first time in two weeks.

While the aerial war between the U.S. and Iran is on pause, the fight for control of the strait, a crucial waterway for global energy exports, continues.

The U.S. is expected to maintain economic pressure on Iran, according to U.S. officials, including sanctions, a blockade of Iranian ports and an effort to seize Iran-linked ships in waters around the world. Even if ship traffic resumes, many analysts believe energy prices will be higher for the rest of the year.

None of that has stopped traders from piling into stocks. With equity investors growing more optimistic that the conflict will end, many have turned their attention instead to companies' first-quarter earnings updates. So far, those reports have painted a relatively upbeat picture of the American economy: Business at the country's biggest banks is booming, and company executives have indicated that consumers are still spending.

The good news continued to roll in this week: Boeing shares rose 5.5% after the plane maker narrowed its quarterly loss and logged brisk commercial-jet sales. Tesla shares jumped post-market after the company beat revenue expectations.

"Investors have come back to the fundamentals, now that the headlines are calming down a bit," said Brian Vendig, chief investment officer at MJP Wealth Advisors.

Rising shares of large tech companies including Microsoft, Amazon and Alphabet juiced gains in the major indexes Wednesday. Those stocks have helped lead the market higher in recent sessions, as investors reconsider the titans of the artificial intelligence trade. Smaller tech names also rose: Micron Technology surged 8.5% Wednesday, while flash memory maker Sandisk Corp jumped 8.4%.

Earlier this year, traders flocked out of richly-priced technology names for stocks in the industrials, materials and similar sectors. The reasoning: those companies could benefit from broadening economic growth — or, in some cases, might be the most insulated from any disruption caused by the advent of AI.

That rotation has been put on pause, and the AI trade is back in the spotlight. Megacap tech companies are still expected to be significant contributors to first-quarter earnings growth. Lower valuations are also luring investors, after the war in Iran and a fresh round of AI jitters battered tech stocks in the first quarter.

"Tech is catching a bid this month because it got beaten up so much in the first few months of the year," Vendig said.

Write to Hannah Erin Lang at hannaherin.lang@wsj.com

data.title: Rollins, Inc. Q1 Sales Increase
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Rollins, Inc. (ROL) released a profit for first quarter of $107.83 million

The company's bottom line came in at $107.83 million, or $0.22 per share. This compares with $105.24 million, or $0.22 per share, last year.

Excluding items, Rollins, Inc. reported adjusted earnings of $113.22 million or $0.24 per share for the period.

The company's revenue for the period rose 10.2% to $906.42 million from $822.50 million last year.

Rollins, Inc. earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $107.83 Mln. vs. $105.24 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $0.22 vs. $0.22 last year.

-Revenue: $906.42 Mln vs. $822.50 Mln last year.
